data.title: Kyocera: to Sell 108.1M Shares of KDDI for Y249.29B
Steel Prices Steady Amid Geopolitical, Trade Uncertainty
data.description:
Steel rebar futures hovered around CNY 2,980 per tonne on Friday, ending the week largely flat as investors weighed mounting geopolitical tensions and economic headwinds.

The market remained cautious after Israel launched a preemptive strike on Iran’s nuclear facilities, prompting retaliatory drone attacks from Tehran.

The sharp escalation heightened global risk aversion, though its immediate impact on steel demand appeared limited.

Meanwhile, uncertainty surrounding US trade policy continued to cloud the outlook as President Donald Trump threatened to impose unilateral tariffs to pressure foreign governments into trade deals.

Meanwhile, the China Iron and Steel Association warned that a deepening price war in the electric vehicle sector is straining steelmakers.

Automakers are reportedly pressuring suppliers to cut prices on steel plates used in vehicle production, further compressing industry margins.

data.title: Indian shipping stocks defy wider weakness as Mideast tensions spur tanker demand hopes
data.description:
** Indian shipping stocks rise, outliers in a weak market

** Great Eastern Shipping Co rises 6%, Shipping Corp of India jumps 10%

** Jump on the day sentiment-driven, on fears Israel-Iran conflict may widen, which could disrupt oil supply and drive up tanker demand and rates - Aishvarya Dadheech, founder and chief executive of Fident Asset Management

** Crude prices have soared on the day, last up more than 9%, dragging local refiners and lifting oil producers

** YTD, GESC up over 7%, SCI up more than 11%

data.title: Talga Group's Sweden Graphite Mine Has 'Monster Potential,' Says Euroz Hartleys
data.description:
Talga Group's Nunasvaara natural graphite mine, which was recently given a permit by the Swedish Government, carries the potential to be a "monster" anode producer, said Euroz Hartleys in a Thursday note.

The company said on Thursday that all appeals against the Nunasvaara South natural graphite mine exploitation concession have been dismissed by the Swedish Government, and all major permits are now in force for the mine.

The company could expand to produce 425,000 tonnes of anode material a year, which would still meet only a small part of future demand, the note added.

The research firm said that the company plans to build an advanced chemical business with an expected 105,000 tonnes per year of anode in 2031.

Euroz Hartleys maintained its speculative buy rating on Talga Group with a price target of AU$1.33.

data.title: Boustead Singapore Explores Sale of Real Estate Assets to Planned REIT; Shares Up 6%
data.description:
Boustead Singapore is considering selling its logistics and industrial real estate assets to a real estate investment trust to be listed on the Singapore Exchange, according to a Singapore bourse filing on Thursday.

The potential sale is part of a strategic review aimed at unlocking shareholder value.

The company said the review remains exploratory, with no assurance that a transaction will materialize.

Shares of the company were up nearly 6% in recent trading.

data.title: Japan's April Industrial Production Falls 1.1%
data.description:
Japan's industrial production fell 1.1% in April from the previous month, government data showed on Friday, as manufacturers trimmed output.

The seasonally adjusted production index stood at 101.3. Inventories dropped 0.8%, while shipments were flat, rising just 0.1%.

Operating ratio rose 1.3%, while production capacity slipped 0.4%.

data.title: Ocado Draws Down $152 Million Letter of Credit Linked to Kroger Partnership
data.description:
Ocado Group said Thursday it has drawn down the $152 million letter of credit issued by the Bank of Nova Scotia, originally established under its 2018 partnership with The Kroger Co.

The capital contribution was tied to the British online supermarket and technology company's investment in Kroger's online business.

The letter of credit had a maturity date of seven years from the signing of the agreement.

data.title: Enity Holding Begins Trading on Nasdaq Stockholm
data.description:
Enity Holding (ENITY.ST) commenced trading on Nasdaq Stockholm on Friday following the completion of a 1.45 billion-kronor initial public offering.

The specialist mortgage company's main shareholder and EQT's EQT VII-indirectly controlled, Butterfly Holdco, sold 25.5 million existing shares priced at 57 kronor apiece. Meanwhile, an overallotment option of up to 3.8 million additional shares was granted to the joint bookrunners, according to a same-day release.

Enity will not receive any primary proceeds from the offering, with settlement scheduled for June 17.

Nordea Bank, Skandinaviska Enskilda Banken, and ABG Sundal Collier acted as joint bookrunners.

data.title: Australia's GemLife finalises A$750 million IPO, term sheet shows
data.description:
Australia's retirement resort group GemLife stock is due to start trading on the Australian Securities Exchange (ASX) on July 3 after finalising its A$750 million ($485.85 million) initial public offering, the largest deal down under this year.

GemLife sold 380.3 million stapled securities in the IPO at A$4.16 each, valuing the company at A$1.58 billion, according to a term sheet reviewed by Reuters.

GemLife declined to comment on the IPO.

The deal is the largest IPO in Australia since DigiCo Infrastructure REIT raised A$2 billion in December.

GemLife's IPO eclipses the high profile Virgin Australia (IPO-VIR.AX) offering, which recently launched its A$685 million bookbuilding process.

About A$725 million worth of the GemLife IPO was sold to cornerstone investors and retail brokers, the term sheet showed.

($1 = 1.5437 Australian dollars)

data.title: Keybridge Receives Board Ouster Notice From Entity Controlled by Former Director
data.description:
Keybridge Capital received a notice from Australian Style Group, an entity controlled by former director Nicholas Bolton, seeking to call a shareholder meeting on June 27 to vote on a resolution to remove four of the company's directors, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The company and major shareholder Wilson Asset Management, which together hold over 50% of shares, intend to vote against the resolution, the filing said.

The company raised procedural issues with the notice, citing insufficient shareholder notice, lack of registry notification to Automic Group, and absence of a telephone dial-in facility.

India considering grounding Air India Boeing 787 fleet after crash, NDTV reports
data.description: The Indian government is considering grounding Air India's Boeing 787 fleet, Indian broadcaster NDTV reported on Friday, a day after one of the airline's aircraft of the same make crashed in Ahmedabad city, killing more than 240 people.

data.title: Bhagwan Marine's The Coral Knight Offers Improved 2026 Economics, Says Euroz Hartleys
data.description:
Bhagwan Marine's The Coral Knight, purchased in the current fiscal half, offers improved economics in 2026 in the context of the overall contribution to the business from the larger boats, said Euroz Hartleys in a Thursday note.

The research firm noted that the company's remaining fleet, a combination of owned and leased vessels, is put to work on a range of opportunities in offshore energy, defense, subsea, decommissioning, and offshore wind.

Euroz Hartleys maintained its buy rating on Bhagwan Marine with a target price of AU$0.93.
